heading: Disclosure requirements for open end consumer credit plans secured by consumer’s principal dwelling
label: 1637a Disclosure requirements for open end consumer credit plans secured by consumer’s principal dwelling
notes: References in TextSection 4 of the Home Equity Loan Consumer Protection Act of 1988, referred to in subsec. (e)(1), is , which is set out as a note below.section 4 of Pub. L. 100–709

(A), inserted dash, substituted “; and” for period at end of subpar. (A), and added subpar. (B).Pub. L. 109–8

Regulations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–8, title XIII, § 1302(c)119 Stat. 209Apr. 20, 2005“(1).—In generalThe Board [of Governors of the Federal Reserve System] shall promulgate regulations implementing the amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 1638, 1664, and 1665b of this title].“(2).—Effective dateRegulations issued under paragraph (1) shall not take effect until the later of—“(A)12 months after the date of enactment of this Act []; orApr. 20, 2005“(B)12 months after the date of publication of such final regulations by the Board.”

Regulations; Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–709, § 7102 Stat. 4734Nov. 23, 1988“(a).—RegulationsBefore the end of the 60-day period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the proposes [sic] of the amendments made by this Act [enacting this section and sections 1647 and 1665b of this title, amending sections 1632 and 1637 of this title, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section and ].Nov. 23, 1988section 1601 of this title“(b).—Effective DateThe amendments made by this Act, and the regulations prescribed pursuant to subsection (a) with respect to such amendments, shall apply to—“(1)any agreement to open an account under an open end consumer credit plan under which extensions of credit are secured by a consumer’s principal dwelling which is entered into after the end of the 5-month period beginning on the date on which the regulations prescribed under subsection (a) become final; and“(2)any application to open such an account which is distributed by, or received by a creditor, after the end of such 5-month period.“(c).—Voluntary ComplianceNotwithstanding subsection (b), any creditor may comply with the amendments made by this Act, in accordance with the regulations prescribed by the Board, before the effective date established under such subsection.”

Consumer Education, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–709, § 4102 Stat. 4733Nov. 23, 1988“The Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System shall develop and prepare a pamphlet for distribution to consumers which contains—“(1)a general description of open end consumer credit plans secured by the consumer’s principal dwelling and the terms and conditions under which such loans are generally extended; and“(2)a discussion of the potential advantages and disadvantages of such plans, including how to compare among home equity plans and between home equity and closed end credit plans.”

children.heading: Fixed annual percentage rate
children.label: 1 Fixed annual percentage rate
children.text: Each annual percentage rate imposed in connection with extensions of credit under the plan and a statement that such rate does not include costs other than interest.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the manner in which such rate will be computed and a statement that such rate does not include costs other than interest;

children.label: i
children.text: any negative amortization and interest rate carryover;

children.label: ii
children.text: the timing of any such changes;

children.label: iii
children.text: any index or margin to which such changes in the rate are related; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a source of information about any such index;

children.label: i
children.text: a statement of such rate and the period of time such initial rate will be in effect; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a statement that such rate does not include costs other than interest;

children.label: D
children.text: a statement that the consumer should ask about the current index value and interest rate;

children.label: E
children.text: a statement of the maximum amount by which the annual percentage rate may change in any 1-year period or a statement that no such limit exists;

children.label: F
children.text: a statement of the maximum annual percentage rate that may be imposed at any time under the plan;

children.label: G
children.text: subject to subsection (b)(3), a table, based on a $10,000 extension of credit, showing how the annual percentage rate and the minimum periodic payment amount under each repayment option of the plan would have been affected during the preceding 15-year period by changes in any index used to compute such rate;

children.label: i
children.text: the maximum annual percentage rate which may be imposed under each repayment option of the plan;

children.label: ii
children.text: the minimum amount of any periodic payment which may be required, based on a $10,000 outstanding balance, under each such option when such maximum annual percentage rate is in effect; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the earliest date by which such maximum annual interest rate may be imposed; and

children.label: I
children.text: a statement that interest rate information will be provided on or with each periodic statement.

children.heading: Other fees imposed by the creditor
children.label: 3 Other fees imposed by the creditor
children.text: An itemization of any fees imposed by the creditor in connection with the availability or use of credit under such plan, including annual fees, application fees, transaction fees, and closing costs (including costs commonly described as “points”), and the time when such fees are payable.

children.heading: Estimates of fees which may be imposed by third parties
children.label: 4 Estimates of fees which may be imposed by third parties

children.heading: Aggregate amount
children.label: A Aggregate amount
children.text: An estimate, based on the creditor’s experience with such plans and stated as a single amount or as a reasonable range, of the aggregate amount of additional fees that may be imposed by third parties (such as governmental authorities, appraisers, and attorneys) in connection with opening an account under the plan.

children.heading: Statement of availability
children.label: B Statement of availability
children.text: A statement that the consumer may ask the creditor for a good faith estimate by the creditor of the fees that may be imposed by third parties.

children.heading: Statement of risk of loss of dwelling
children.label: 5 Statement of risk of loss of dwelling
A
children.text: any extension of credit under the plan is secured by the consumer’s dwelling; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the event of any default, the consumer risks the loss of the dwelling.

children.heading: Conditions to which disclosed terms are subject
children.label: 6 Conditions to which disclosed terms are subject

children.heading: Period during which such terms are available
children.label: A Period during which such terms are available
i
children.text: of the time by which an application must be submitted to obtain the terms disclosed; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if applicable, that the terms are subject to change.

children.heading: Right of refusal if certain terms change
children.label: B Right of refusal if certain terms change
i
children.text: the consumer may elect not to enter into an agreement to open an account under the plan if any term changes (other than a change contemplated by a variable feature of the plan) before any such agreement is final; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the consumer makes an election described in clause (i), the consumer is entitled to a refund of all fees paid in connection with the application.

children.heading: Retention of information
children.label: C Retention of information
children.text: A statement that the consumer should make or otherwise retain a copy of information disclosed under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Rights of creditor with respect to extensions of credit
children.label: 7 Rights of creditor with respect to extensions of credit
A
children.text: under certain conditions, the creditor may terminate any account under the plan and require immediate repayment of any outstanding balance, prohibit any additional extension of credit to the account, or reduce the credit limit applicable to the account; and

children.label: B
children.text: the consumer may receive, upon request, more specific information about the conditions under which the creditor may take any action described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Repayment options and minimum periodic payments
children.label: 8 Repayment options and minimum periodic payments
i
children.text: any period during which additional extensions of credit may be obtained; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any period during which repayment is required to be made and no additional extensions of credit may be obtained;

children.label: B
children.text: the length of any repayment period, including any differences in the length of any repayment period with regard to the periods described in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A); and

children.label: C
children.text: an explanation of how the amount of any minimum monthly or periodic payment will be determined under each such option, including any differences in the determination of any such amount with regard to the periods described in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Example of minimum payments and maximum repayment period
children.label: 9 Example of minimum payments and maximum repayment period
children.text: An example, based on a $10,000 outstanding balance and the interest rate (other than a rate not based on the index under the plan) which is, or was recently, in effect under such plan, showing the minimum monthly or periodic payment, and the time it would take to repay the entire $10,000 if the consumer paid only the minimum periodic payments and obtained no additional extensions of credit.

children.label: A
children.text: would not repay any of the principal balance; or

children.label: B
children.text: would repay less than the outstanding balance by the end of such period,

children.label: A
children.text: any limitation in the plan on the amount of any increase in the minimum payments may result in negative amortization;

children.label: B
children.text: negative amortization increases the outstanding principal balance of the account; and

children.label: C
children.text: negative amortization reduces the consumer’s equity in the consumer’s dwelling.

children.heading: Limitations and minimum amount requirements on extensions of credit
children.label: 12 Limitations and minimum amount requirements on extensions of credit

children.heading: Number and dollar amount limitations
children.label: A Number and dollar amount limitations
children.text: Any limitation contained in the plan on the number of extensions of credit and the amount of credit which may be obtained during any month or other defined time period.

children.heading: Minimum balance and other transaction amount requirements
children.label: B Minimum balance and other transaction amount requirements
i
children.text: the initial extension of credit to an account under the plan;

children.label: ii
children.text: any subsequent extension of credit to an account under the plan; or

children.label: iii
children.text: any outstanding balance of an account under the plan.

children.label: A
children.text: the consumer should consult a tax advisor regarding the deductibility of interest and charges under the plan; and

children.label: B
children.text: in any case in which the extension of credit exceeds the fair market value (as defined under title 26) of the dwelling, the interest on the portion of the credit extension that is greater than the fair market value of the dwelling is not tax deductible for Federal income tax purposes.

children.heading: Disclosure requirements established by Bureau
children.label: 14 Disclosure requirements established by Bureau
children.text: Any other term which the Bureau requires, in regulations, to be disclosed.

children.heading: Time and form of disclosures
children.label: b Time and form of disclosures

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The disclosures required under subsection (a) with respect to any open end consumer credit plan which provides for any extension of credit which is secured by the consumer’s principal dwelling and the pamphlet required under subsection (e) shall be provided to any consumer at the time the creditor distributes an application to establish an account under such plan to such consumer.

children.heading: Telephone, publications, and third party applications
children.label: B Telephone, publications, and third party applications
children.text: In the case of telephone applications, applications contained in magazines or other publications, or applications provided by a third party, the disclosures required under subsection (a) and the pamphlet required under subsection (e) shall be provided by the creditor before the end of the 3-day period beginning on the date the creditor receives a completed application from a consumer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (1)(B), the disclosures required under subsection (a) shall be provided on or with any application to establish an account under an open end consumer credit plan which provides for any extension of credit which is secured by the consumer’s principal dwelling.

children.heading: Segregation of required disclosures from other information
children.label: B Segregation of required disclosures from other information
children.text: The disclosures required under subsection (a) shall be conspicuously segregated from all other terms, data, or additional information provided in connection with the application, either by grouping the disclosures separately on the application form or by providing the disclosures on a separate form, in accordance with regulations of the Bureau.

children.heading: Precedence of certain information
children.label: C Precedence of certain information
children.text: The disclosures required by paragraphs (5), (6), and (7) of subsection (a) shall precede all of the other required disclosures.

children.heading: Special provision relating to variable interest rate information
children.label: D Special provision relating to variable interest rate information
children.text: Whether or not the disclosures required under subsection (a) are provided on the application form, the variable rate information described in subsection (a)(2) may be provided separately from the other information required to be disclosed.

children.heading: Requirement for historical table
children.label: 3 Requirement for historical table
children.text: In preparing the table required under subsection (a)(2)(G), the creditor shall consistently select one rate of interest for each year and the manner of selecting the rate from year to year shall be consistent with the plan.

children.label: A
children.text: the disclosures required under subsection (a) with respect to such plan, in accordance with subsection (b); and

children.label: B
children.text: the pamphlet required under subsection (e); or

if such person cannot provide specific terms about the plan because specific information about the plan terms is not available, no nonrefundable fee may be imposed in connection with such application before the end of the 3-day period beginning on the date the consumer receives the disclosures required under subsection (a) with respect to the application.

children.heading: “Principal dwelling” defined
children.label: d “Principal dwelling” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section and sections 1647 and 1665b of this title, the term “principal dwelling” includes any second or vacation home of the consumer.
a pamphlet published by the Bureau pursuant to section 4 of the Home Equity Consumer Protection Act of 1988; or1So in original. Probably should be followed by “Loan”.1
any pamphlet which provides substantially similar information to the information described in such section, as determined by the Bureau.
